qt: In recent years, the influence of personality variables on ______ styles has increased greatly.
Learning styles and strategies are often ______.
Cognitive-style research initially addressed phenomena as perceptual speed & flexibility; later researchers focused on processing styles from the point of view of ______ psychology.
The domain that helps analyzing the language, drawing inferences and categorizing new knowledge is termed as _______ domain.
In second language acquisition process, the factor that determines how much a learner wants to learn a second language is termed as ______.
Recent preferred learning models include multiple intelligence models, _________ model.
Ehrman and Leaver (2001-2003) have reorganized the scales for cognitive styles like ________.
Researchers use learning style research with ________ & cognitive styles to determine ability, predict performance, and improve classroom teaching and learning.
The domain which involves the learners’ self and encourages them to develop personal relevance to L2 and gives importance to their feelings is termed as _______ domain.
The factors related to the environment or social context within which the learner of second language is grounded are termed as ________ factors.
Language awareness pedagogical theory works in ________ domains.
The _______ domain is the main domain in the second language learning and within it the role of the first language stands out very clearly.
The domain which fosters tolerance and acceptance of ethnic diversity in second language classrooms is called ________ domain.
The domain which allows the learner to use the second language more effectively by raising consciousness of how a language is used & a word can have multiple meanings is termed as ________ domain.
The people or learners who are introvert do not communicate much & find it difficult to practice the second language. This factor is related to the learner's ______.
The use of L1 in L2 classrooms has been appraised as too complex for learners, requiring them to operate in _______ different ways.
The use of the first language in the ________ domain leads to develop cross cultural and cross linguistic links.
According to ________, assigning a role to L1 in L2 learning provides the learners with the opportunity to view language objectively as a phenomenon.
The factors related to the personality of the learner of a second language are called ________ factors.
Researchers argue that it is easy for the children as compared to adult learners to learn a second language. This factor is related to _______ .
Which of the following medium oriented strategies should not only be allowed but actively encouraged to ensure real communicative competence?
In defense of the use of first language, ______ (1999) says that it is not really possible to stop the flow of the first language in the learner’s mind.
The domain which creates awareness of the way language can be used to imply meaning is termed as _________ domain.
In early development stages, the learners have to resort to ________ language, the only available resource to organize and process new information.
First language maintains the vital ________ relationship between speech and thought.
L1 as Semiotic Budget is an inevitable resource in learning L2.
Language awareness pedagogical theory is suggested as complementary to _______ language teaching for its unique stance between L1 and L2.
In communicative language teaching, as the sole focus is on meaning processing, learners often fail to notice the difference in form, leading to ______ of errors.
Language awareness sees integral links between L1 and L2 and strongly stresses the _______ role of L1 in L2 learning.
According to sociocultural theory, ____ speech is the third fundamental tool in learning conceptualized
Language awareness believes that explicit instruction and focus on __________ leads to L2 acquisition.
The only tool that remains available is ______ as the learners struggle to learn L2.
Use of L1 in L2 classrooms enables the learners to make learning more _______.
Humans interact through signs which are _______ & historically determined.
Learners’ ‘agency’ is embedded in their _______ histories.
Sociocultural theory emphasizes that there is a crucial link between ________ processes and sociocultural settings.
Drawing on L1, Language Awareness focuses on forms & functions of ________ language by exploiting a methodology that is reflexive, inductive, interactive & investigational.
Language awareness empowers the learners to consolidate & systemize knowledge of L2 and become conscious of the relationship between _______ languages.
Language awareness is predicated on the assumption of _______ between acquisition and learning.
Language awareness accords value to learners’ _______ background and their L1, and capitalizes learners’ knowledge & experience of L1.
Learners are more likely to switch to L1 in their ________ speech when confronted with a difficult task.
Language awareness emphasizes a person’s sensitivity and _______ awareness of language’s nature and role in human life.
